systemPrompt:
# SYSTEM INFORMATION
OS: macOS
Shell: /bin/zsh
Home Path: /Users/user
CWD: /Users/user/Desktop/SR
This is the CWD, and where tools default to. When using tools, use relative paths, not absolute
====
# CWD RULES
- **Identity**: You are Jarvis, an senior agentic assistant, doing agentic tasks for users. You are a god at problem solving, coding, general case-all tasks and what not. You speak with authortitative confidence, no AI-nxiety. Lastly, keep things concise as well but expand when neccessary.
- **Workspace**: Operations for task & tools are anchored at `/Users/user/Desktop/SR` All paths must be relative, its a waste of tokens calling absolute paths. For example when calling a path for a tool in root, lets say reading a file, call sample.txt, NOT `/Users/user/Desktop/SR/sample.txt`
- **Commands**: Tailor commands to darwin
- **Context**: Use search tools to narrow down target areas, then read specific files or line ranges as needed. If you write a file, in the result you will get an automatic read for it in the result, which means you dont need to re-read files after writing them. For any edit you do, if you have a file in context already, reads are automatically refreshed once you make an edit! Refer back to the edit result's previous content section to see what the previous content was!
====
# CURRENT MODE
- **Mode:** Code
- **Model:** kilo-auto/free
- **Persona:** You are Jarvis, an elite software engineer. You write perfect, bug-free code on the first try. You verify your own work. You do not need to be hand-held. You are the one who knocks.
- **Language:** English
- **Mode Instructions**: 1. Execute tasks with precision and efficiency. Focus on high-quality implementation.

2. Provide brief, high-level context for your actions, explaining the 'why' behind architectural decisions or complex changes.

3. Minimize conversational filler. You are here to ship code, not to chat. 

4. If a task is complex, outline your technical approach briefly before execution.
====
## TOOL USAGE:
@tool DSL SCHEMA
===
Description:
@read: read files. One file per line. Ranges are optional. Use start-end for line ranges, Hn for the first n lines, and Tn for the last n lines.
@grep: search text. Case-insensitive by default. Use | for multiple queries. Optional path or include=glob list. Query text is plain text only. This is code-first search, so common noise such as docs, text files, assets, generated files, and locales may be excluded unless you opt in with include=.
@find: find files or patterns. Use | for multiple names or patterns. Optional path.
@ls: list files.
@bash: run a shell command. Optional path.
@web: search the web.
@fetch: fetch a URL.
Agent: delegate a focused task to a sub-agent.
@edit: edit files. Multi-block edits are preferred. Partial edits can go through in multi-block edits when blocks do not overlap.
@write: write a full file.
Todo: set or replace the todo list with markdown checklist items.
===

Syntax:
@read: "path[ranges?]"
@grep: "query|optional pipes?" "include=*.ts,*.tsx|optional path?" (include= is optional; split with | if you need both include and path. Use include= when searching text/docs/non-code files)
@find: "pattern,optional commas?" "optional path?"
@ls: "path"
@bash: "command" "optional path?"
@web: "query"
@fetch: "url"
@agent: "prompt"
@edit: "path"
otxt[range?]: Old content
ntxt: New content

[More otxt/ntxt blocks?]
etxt
@write: "path"
Content goes here
etxt

@todo: "title?"
[ ] item
[-] item
[x] item
ETXT
===

Examples:
@read: "sample.txt"    
@read: "sample.txt[1-7,10-19,T20]"
@read: "sample.txt[1-100]"
@grep: "dog|cats|pizza" "include=*.txt|sample.txt"
@grep: "AuthService|SessionManager" "include=*.ts,*.tsx"
@grep: "readtool|edittool" "include=*.tsx|webview-ui"
@find: "*.ts,*.tsx" "src"
@find: "tsconfig.json,package.json"
@ls: "."
@ls: "src/components"
@bash: "echo all your base are belong to us"
@bash: "npm run build" "react-app"
@web: "best pizza in nyc"
@fetch: "https://example.com"

@agent: "analyze the current project structure"
@edit: "sample.txt"
otxt[4-6]: Line 2
Line 3
ntxt: Edited line 2
Edited line 3
Added line 4

otxt: Bad line
ntxt: Good line

otxt: the tenth and last line.
ntxt: cool!
etxt
@write: "sample.txt"
This is a sample text file
This is line 2 of this sample text file
etxt

@todo: "Implementation"
[ ] Analyze requirements
[-] Update parser
[x] Add tests
ETXT

Simple independent batch example:
@read: "README.md[1-40]"
@grep: "TODO|FIXME" "src"
@write: "notes.txt"
Investigation notes
- README intro captured separately
- TODO/FIXME scan requested separately
etxt
===

Execution & Rules:
- Use prose when no tools are needed.
- Use only tool calls when tools are needed.
- Tool mode begins when the first non-whitespace line starts with @name:
- Tool lines start at column 1 as @name:
- Only a top-level line starting at column 1 as @name: is a tool call.
Invald: Let me read this file @read: "file.ts"
Valid: Let me read this file
@read: "file.ts"
=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.
- If you are discussing, explaining, or showing the tool syntax itself, always escape those tool lines and etxt/EOF markers.
=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.
- When history shows either form, treat the paired tool result as the source of truth for what changed.
=== Batching
Batching: multiple tool calls allowed.

Rules:
- Only use the tools listed here.
- Only the tool names listed here are valid top-level tool names.
- Do not invent syntax.
- Do not add unsupported values not shown in the syntax, and do not invent --flags; only use the argument shapes shown here. The only tool that flags are allowed in is the @bash tool.
- Do not mix prose with tool calls in the same response.
- Do not include prose after tool calls.
- The only thing allowed after a tool call, is another tool call or the end of the response.
- If you include prose after a tool call, the response will be rejected.
- @grep is optimized for code search, not raw grep. If the target may be in .txt, .md, docs, assets, generated files, or similar non-code content, add an explicit include= glob that matches those files.
- Read before edit unless the exact target and/or context is already in recent tool results.
- Batch independent calls only.
- Do not batch dependent calls. Such as editing a file in the same turn as reading but that read isnt even in your context yet, so you're assuming what the contents are.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery.
- Stop using tools when the task is complete.
===

Flow:
- Map -> @ls or @find
- Search -> @grep (grep is your most important tool for discovery and likely will get you to where you need to be 99% of the time)
- Read -> @read (prefer specific line ranges over full file reads)
- Change or identify -> Identify/deduce problem, @edit, or @write the solution

27→async def broadcast_ws():
28→    """Push latest machine data to all WebSocket clients immediately."""
29→    if not active_connections:
30→        return
31→    db = database.SessionLocal()
32→    try:
33→        machines = db.query(models.Machine).all()
34→        data = []
35→        for m in machines:
36→            remaining = max(0.0, m.initial_asnova_length - (m.current_total_meters - m.meters_at_fill))
37→            now = datetime.utcnow()
38→            is_esp_online = m.last_seen and (now - m.last_seen).total_seconds() < 60
39→            if not is_esp_online:
40→                status = "OFFLINE"
41→            elif m.current_baud == 0:
42→                status = "ESP_ONLINE_NO_SIGNAL"
43→            elif remaining <= 0:
44→                status = "ASNOVA_EMPTY"
45→            else:
46→                status = "RUNNING"
47→            data.append({
48→                "id": m.id, "status": status,
49→                "meters": round(m.current_total_meters, 2),
50→                "shift_meters": round(m.shift_meters, 2),
51→                "remaining": round(remaining, 2),
52→                "baud": m.current_baud, "protocol": m.current_protocol,
53→                "connection_source": m.connection_source,
54→                "preferred_source": m.preferred_source,
55→                "sys_info": {
56→                    "ram": {"free": m.esp_free_ram, "total": m.esp_total_ram},
57→                    "rom": {"free": m.esp_free_rom, "total": m.esp_total_rom},
58→                    "cpu": m.esp_cpu_freq,
59→                    "wifi": {"ssid": m.esp_wifi_ssid, "rssi": m.esp_wifi_rssi}
60→                }
61→            })
62→        msg = json.dumps(data)
63→        for ws in list(active_connections):
64→            try:
65→                await ws.send_text(msg)
66→            except:
67→                active_connections.remove(ws)
68→    finally:
69→        db.close()
70→
71→def broadcast_priority(machine_id: str):
72→    """Sends a UDP broadcast to the ESP32 mesh gateway."""
73→    try:
74→        sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
75→        sock.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_BROADCAST, 1)
76→        interfaces = ["192.168.1.255", "192.168.4.255"]
77→        message = f"PRIORITY:{machine_id}".encode()
78→        for ip in interfaces:
79→            sock.sendto(message, (ip, 4444))
80→        sock.close()
81→    except Exception as e:
82→        print(f"UDP Broadcast Error: {e}")

118→# ─── Helpers ───
119→def get_password_hash(password):
120→    return bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode('utf-8'), bcrypt.gensalt()).decode('utf-8')
121→
122→def verify_password(plain_password, hashed_password):
123→    return bcrypt.checkpw(plain_password.encode('utf-8'), hashed_password.encode('utf-8'))
124→
125→def create_access_token(data: dict, expires_delta: timedelta = None):
126→    to_encode = data.copy()
127→    expire = datetime.utcnow() + (expires_delta or timedelta(minutes=15))
128→    to_encode.update({"exp": expire})
129→    return jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)
130→
131→async def get_current_user(token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme), db: Session = Depends(database.get_db)):
132→    credentials_exception = HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="Could not validate credentials", headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"})
133→    try:
134→        payload = jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
135→        username: str = payload.get("sub")
136→        if username is None:
137→            raise credentials_exception
138→    except JWTError:
139→        raise credentials_exception
140→    user = db.query(models.User).filter(models.User.username == username).first()
141→    if user is None:
142→        raise credentials_exception
143→    return user
144→
145→def require_role(*roles):
146→    def checker(current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
147→        if current_user.role not in roles:
148→            raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Insufficient permissions")
149→        return current_user
150→    return checker
151→
152→def get_week_start(d: date = None) -> str:
153→    """Get Monday of the week containing date d."""
154→    if d is None:
155→        d = date.today()
156→    monday = d - timedelta(days=d.weekday())
157→    return monday.isoformat()

161→async def login_for_access_token(form_data: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm = Depends(), db: Session = Depends(database.get_db)):
162→    user = db.query(models.User).filter(models.User.username == form_data.username).first()
163→    if not user or not verify_password(form_data.password, user.password_hash):
164→        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED, detail="Incorrect username or password", headers={"WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer"})
165→    access_token = create_access_token(data={"sub": user.username}, expires_delta=timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES))
166→    return {"access_token": access_token, "token_type": "bearer"}

193→    user = db.query(models.User).filter(models.User.id == user_id).first()
194→    if not user:
195→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="User not found")
196→    if data.full_name is not None:
197→        user.full_name = data.full_name
198→    if data.role is not None:
199→        user.role = data.role
200→    if data.password is not None:
201→        user.password_hash = get_password_hash(data.password)
202→    if data.is_active is not None:
203→        user.is_active = data.is_active
204→    db.commit()
205→    db.refresh(user)
206→    return user
207→
208→@app.delete("/users/{user_id}")
209→async def delete_user(user_id: int, db: Session = Depends(database.get_db), current_user: models.User = Depends(require_role(models.UserRole.ADMIN, models.UserRole.NAZORATCHI))):
210→    user = db.query(models.User).filter(models.User.id == user_id).first()
211→    if not user:
212→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="User not found")
213→    if user.id == current_user.id:
214→        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Cannot delete yourself")
215→    if current_user.role == models.UserRole.NAZORATCHI and user.role == models.UserRole.ADMIN:
216→        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Nazoratchi cannot delete admin users")
217→    db.delete(user)
218→    db.commit()
219→    return {"message": "User deleted"}

263→    assignments = db.query(models.Assignment).filter(models.Assignment.is_active == 1).all()
264→    result = []
265→    for a in assignments:
266→        result.append(schemas.AssignmentOut(
267→                    id=a.id, operator_id=a.operator_id, machine_id=a.machine_id,
268→                    shift_type=a.shift_type, is_active=a.is_active, assigned_at=a.assigned_at,
269→                    operator_name=a.operator.name if a.operator else ""
270→                ))

272→
273→@app.post("/assignments", response_model=List[schemas.AssignmentOut])
274→async def create_assignment(data: schemas.AssignmentCreate, db: Session = Depends(database.get_db), current_user: models.User = Depends(require_role(models.UserRole.ADMIN, models.UserRole.MASTER))):
275→    # Get operator's shift_type automatically
276→    operator = db.query(models.Operator).filter(models.Operator.id == data.operator_id).first()
277→    if not operator:
278→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Operator not found")
279→    shift = operator.shift_type
280→    results = []
281→    for mid in data.machine_ids:
282→        # Deactivate existing active assignment for this machine IN THIS SHIFT only
283→        existing = db.query(models.Assignment).filter(
284→            models.Assignment.machine_id == mid,
285→            models.Assignment.shift_type == shift,
286→            models.Assignment.is_active == 1
287→        ).all()
288→        for e in existing:
289→            e.is_active = 0
290→        a = models.Assignment(operator_id=data.operator_id, machine_id=mid, shift_type=shift)
291→        db.add(a)
292→        db.flush()
293→        results.append(schemas.AssignmentOut(id=a.id, operator_id=a.operator_id, machine_id=a.machine_id, shift_type=a.shift_type, is_active=a.is_active, assigned_at=a.assigned_at, operator_name=""))
294→    db.commit()
295→    return results
296→
297→@app.delete("/assignments/{assignment_id}")
298→async def delete_assignment(assignment_id: int, db: Session = Depends(database.get_db), current_user: models.User = Depends(require_role(models.UserRole.ADMIN, models.UserRole.MASTER))):
299→    a = db.query(models.Assignment).filter(models.Assignment.id == assignment_id).first()
300→    if not a:
301→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Assignment not found")
302→    a.is_active = 0
303→    db.commit()
304→    return {"message": "Assignment removed"}

319→@app.post("/rest-days")

321→    # Toggle: if exists, remove; if not, add
322→    existing = db.query(models.RestDay).filter(
323→        models.RestDay.operator_id == data.operator_id,
324→        models.RestDay.day_of_week == data.day_of_week,
325→        models.RestDay.week_start == data.week_start
326→    ).first()
327→    if existing:
328→        db.delete(existing)
329→        db.commit()
330→        return {"message": "Rest day removed", "action": "removed"}
331→    else:
332→        rd = models.RestDay(operator_id=data.operator_id, day_of_week=data.day_of_week, week_start=data.week_start, created_by=current_user.id)
333→        db.add(rd)
334→        db.commit()
335→        return {"message": "Rest day set", "action": "added"}

346→# ─── MACHINES ───
347→@app.get("/machines")
348→async def read_machines(db: Session = Depends(database.get_db), current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
349→    machines = db.query(models.Machine).all()
350→    results = []
351→    now = datetime.utcnow()
352→    for m in machines:
353→        remaining = max(0.0, m.initial_asnova_length - (m.current_total_meters - m.meters_at_fill))
354→        is_esp_online = m.last_seen and (now - m.last_seen).total_seconds() < 60
355→        if not is_esp_online:
356→            status = "OFFLINE"
357→        elif m.current_baud == 0:
358→            status = "ESP_ONLINE_NO_SIGNAL"
359→        elif remaining <= 0:
360→            status = "ASNOVA_EMPTY"
361→        else:
362→            status = "RUNNING"
363→        results.append({
364→            "id": m.id, "status": status,
365→            "category_id": m.category_id,
366→            "meters": round(m.current_total_meters, 2),
367→            "shift_meters": round(m.shift_meters, 2),
368→            "remaining": round(remaining, 2),
369→            "initial_asnova_length": m.initial_asnova_length,
370→            "meters_at_fill": m.meters_at_fill,
371→            "baud": m.current_baud, "protocol": m.current_protocol,
372→            "connection_source": m.connection_source,
373→            "preferred_source": m.preferred_source,
374→            "last_seen": m.last_seen.isoformat() if m.last_seen else None,
375→            "sys_info": {
376→                "ram": {"free": m.esp_free_ram, "total": m.esp_total_ram},
377→                "rom": {"free": m.esp_free_rom, "total": m.esp_total_rom},
378→                "cpu": m.esp_cpu_freq,
379→                "wifi": {"ssid": m.esp_wifi_ssid, "rssi": m.esp_wifi_rssi}
380→            }
381→        })
382→    return results
383→@app.post("/machines/{machine_id}/fill")
384→async def fill_asnova(machine_id: str, data: schemas.MachineUpdate, db: Session = Depends(database.get_db), current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
385→    machine = db.query(models.Machine).filter(models.Machine.id == machine_id).first()
386→    if not machine:
387→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Machine not found")
388→    machine.initial_asnova_length = data.initial_asnova_length
389→    machine.meters_at_fill = machine.current_total_meters
390→    machine.last_operator_id = data.last_operator_id
391→    machine.status = "RUNNING"
392→    new_log = models.AsnovaLog(machine_id=machine_id, operator_id=data.last_operator_id, operator_name=current_user.full_name, length_added=data.initial_asnova_length, meters_at_fill=machine.meters_at_fill)
393→    db.add(new_log)
394→    db.commit()
395→    await broadcast_ws()
396→    return {"message": "Asnova filled successfully"}
397→
398→@app.post("/machines/{machine_id}/update")
399→async def update_machine_data(machine_id: str, data: MachineUpdate):
400→    db = database.SessionLocal()
401→    try:
402→        machine = db.query(models.Machine).filter(models.Machine.id == machine_id).first()
403→        if not machine:
404→            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Machine not found")
405→        if machine.preferred_source == "USB" and (datetime.utcnow() - machine.last_seen).total_seconds() < 10:
406→            return {"message": "WiFi ignored (USB preferred)"}
407→        machine.current_total_meters = data.meters
408→        machine.shift_meters = data.shift_meters
409→        machine.current_baud = data.baud
410→        machine.current_protocol = data.protocol
411→        machine.last_seen = datetime.utcnow()
412→        machine.connection_source = "WIFI"
413→        machine.esp_free_ram = data.free_ram
414→        machine.esp_total_ram = data.total_ram
415→        machine.esp_free_rom = data.free_rom
416→        machine.esp_total_rom = data.total_rom
417→        machine.esp_cpu_freq = data.cpu_freq
418→        machine.esp_wifi_ssid = data.wifi_ssid
419→        machine.esp_wifi_rssi = data.wifi_rssi
420→        db.commit()
421→        await broadcast_ws()
422→        return {"message": "Data updated"}
423→    finally:
424→        db.close()
425→

470→# ─── PDF REPORTS ───
471→@app.get("/reports/daily/pdf")
472→async def get_daily_report_pdf(db: Session = Depends(database.get_db), current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
473→    """Daily shift report: meters per machine."""
474→    today = datetime.utcnow().date()
475→    machines = db.query(models.Machine).all()
476→    rows = []
477→    total = 0.0
478→    for m in machines:
479→        meters = round(m.shift_meters, 1)
480→        rows.append([m.id, f"{meters} m"])
481→        total += meters
482→    rows.append(["JAMI", f"{total:.1f} m"])
483→    file_obj = reports.generate_pdf_report("KUNLIK SMENA HISOBOTI", ["Stanok", "Metr"], rows)
484→    return StreamingResponse(file_obj, media_type="application/pdf", headers={"Content-Disposition": "attachment; filename=daily_report.pdf"})
485→
486→@app.get("/reports/monthly/pdf")
487→async def get_monthly_report_pdf(db: Session = Depends(database.get_db), current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
488→    """Monthly report: per operator, per day meters."""
489→    now = datetime.utcnow()
490→    start_of_month = datetime(now.year, now.month, 1)
491→    logs = db.query(models.ProductionLog).filter(models.ProductionLog.date >= start_of_month).all()
492→    
493→    # Group by operator
494→    op_data = {}
495→    for l in logs:
496→        name = l.operator_name or "Unknown"
497→        if name not in op_data:
498→            op_data[name] = {"machine": l.machine_id, "days": {}, "total": 0.0}
499→        day_str = l.date.strftime("%d-%m")
500→        op_data[name]["days"][day_str] = op_data[name]["days"].get(day_str, 0) + l.meters_woven
501→        op_data[name]["total"] += l.meters_woven
502→    
503→    rows = []
504→    for name, info in op_data.items():
505→        rows.append([name, info["machine"], ""])
506→        for day, meters in sorted(info["days"].items()):
507→            rows.append(["", f"  {day}", f"{meters:.0f} m"])
508→        rows.append(["", "JAMI", f"{info['total']:.0f} m"])
509→    
510→    if not rows:
511→        rows = [["Ma'lumot yo'q", "", ""]]
512→    
513→    file_obj = reports.generate_pdf_report("OYLIK OPERATOR HISOBOTI", ["Operator", "Kun/Stankok", "Metr"], rows)
514→    return StreamingResponse(file_obj, media_type="application/pdf", headers={"Content-Disposition": "attachment; filename=monthly_report.pdf"})
515→
516→@app.get("/reports/monitoring/pdf")
517→async def get_monitoring_pdf(db: Session = Depends(database.get_db), current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
518→    machines = db.query(models.Machine).all()
519→    data = []
520→    for m in machines:
521→        remaining = max(0.0, m.initial_asnova_length - (m.current_total_meters - m.meters_at_fill))
522→        data.append([m.id, m.status, f"{m.current_total_meters:.2f}", f"{remaining:.2f}"])
523→    file_obj = reports.generate_pdf_report("Machine Monitoring Report", ["Machine ID", "Status", "Meters", "Remaining"], data)
524→    return StreamingResponse(file_obj, media_type="application/pdf", headers={"Content-Disposition": "attachment; filename=monitoring_report.pdf"})
525→
526→class CustomPDFRequest(BaseModel):
527→    title: str
528→    filename: str
529→    rows: int
530→    cols: int
531→    cells: dict
532→
533→@app.post("/reports/generate-pdf")
534→async def generate_custom_pdf(data: CustomPDFRequest, current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
535→    """Generate PDF from custom Excel-like table data and save it."""
536→    safe_filename = "".join(c for c in data.filename if c.isalnum() or c in (' ', '-', '_')).strip()
537→    if not safe_filename:
538→        safe_filename = "hisobot"
539→    
540→    pdf_bytes = reports.generate_custom_pdf(data.title, data.rows, data.cols, data.cells)
541→    
542→    # Save PDF to disk
543→    pdf_id = str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
544→    pdf_path = os.path.join(SAVED_PDFS_DIR, f"{safe_filename}_{pdf_id}.pdf")
545→    with open(pdf_path, 'wb') as f:
546→        f.write(pdf_bytes.read())
547→    
548→    # Save metadata
549→    meta_path = os.path.join(SAVED_PDFS_DIR, f"{safe_filename}_{pdf_id}.json")
550→    import json
551→    meta = {
552→        "id": pdf_id,
553→        "title": data.title,
554→        "filename": safe_filename,
555→        "rows": data.rows,
556→        "cols": data.cols,
557→        "cells": data.cells,
558→        "pdf_path": pdf_path,
559→        "created_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
560→        "created_by": current_user.full_name
561→    }
562→    with open(meta_path, 'w') as f:
563→        json.dump(meta, f, ensure_ascii=False)
564→    
565→    # Return PDF for download
566→    with open(pdf_path, 'rb') as f:
567→        pdf_content = f.read()
568→    
569→    return StreamingResponse(io.BytesIO(pdf_content), media_type="application/pdf", 
570→                           headers={"Content-Disposition": f"attachment; filename={safe_filename}.pdf"})
571→
572→
573→@app.get("/reports/saved")
574→async def list_saved_pdfs(current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
575→    """List all saved PDFs."""
576→    import json
577→    result = []
578→    if not os.path.exists(SAVED_PDFS_DIR):
579→        return result
580→    for fname in sorted(os.listdir(SAVED_PDFS_DIR)):
581→        if fname.endswith('.json'):
582→            try:
583→                with open(os.path.join(SAVED_PDFS_DIR, fname), 'r') as f:
584→                    meta = json.load(f)
585→                    # Don't send cells data in list to keep response small
586→                    result.append({
587→                        "id": meta.get("id"),
588→                        "title": meta.get("title"),
589→                        "filename": meta.get("filename"),
590→                        "rows": meta.get("rows"),
591→                        "cols": meta.get("cols"),
592→                        "created_at": meta.get("created_at"),
593→                        "created_by": meta.get("created_by")
594→                    })
595→            except Exception:
596→                pass
597→    return result
598→

600→@app.get("/reports/saved/{pdf_id}/download")
601→async def download_saved_pdf(pdf_id: str, current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
602→    """Download a saved PDF."""
603→    import json
604→    for fname in os.listdir(SAVED_PDFS_DIR):
605→        if fname.endswith('.json'):
606→            try:
607→                with open(os.path.join(SAVED_PDFS_DIR, fname), 'r') as f:
608→                    meta = json.load(f)
609→                    if meta.get("id") == pdf_id:
610→                        pdf_path = meta.get("pdf_path")
611→                        if pdf_path and os.path.exists(pdf_path):
612→                            return FileResponse(pdf_path, media_type="application/pdf",
613→                                              filename=f"{meta.get('filename', 'hisobot')}.pdf")
614→            except Exception:
615→                pass
616→    raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="PDF not found")

643→
644→@app.delete("/reports/saved/{pdf_id}")
645→async def delete_saved_pdf(pdf_id: str, current_user: models.User = Depends(get_current_user)):
646→    """Delete a saved PDF."""
647→    import json
648→    to_delete = []
649→    for fname in os.listdir(SAVED_PDFS_DIR):
650→        if fname.endswith('.json'):
651→            try:
652→                with open(os.path.join(SAVED_PDFS_DIR, fname), 'r') as f:
653→                    meta = json.load(f)
654→                    if meta.get("id") == pdf_id:
655→                        to_delete.append(os.path.join(SAVED_PDFS_DIR, fname))
656→                        if meta.get("pdf_path") and os.path.exists(meta["pdf_path"]):
657→                            to_delete.append(meta["pdf_path"])
658→            except Exception:
659→                pass
660→    for path in to_delete:
661→        try:
662→            os.remove(path)
663→        except Exception:
664→            pass
665→    return {"message": "PDF deleted"}
666→
667→# ─── SERIAL MONITORING THREAD ───
668→def serial_monitor_task():
669→    serial_port = "/dev/cu.usbmodem5C361404121"
670→    while True:
671→        try:
672→            import serial
673→            with serial.Serial(serial_port, 115200, timeout=1) as ser:
674→                print(f"Serial: Connected to {serial_port}", flush=True)
675→                while True:
676→                    try:
677→                        line = ser.readline().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore').strip()
678→                        if not line:
679→                            continue
680→                        if line.startswith("USB_DATA:"):
681→                            payload_str = line[9:]
682→                            data = json.loads(payload_str)
683→                            db = database.SessionLocal()
684→                            try:
685→                                m = db.query(models.Machine).filter(models.Machine.id == data['node_id']).first()
686→                                if m and m.preferred_source != "WIFI":
687→                                    m.current_total_meters = data['meters']
688→                                    m.shift_meters = data['shift_meters']
689→                                    m.current_baud = data['baud']
690→                                    m.current_protocol = data['protocol']
691→                                    m.last_seen = datetime.utcnow()
692→                                    m.connection_source = "USB"
693→                                    m.esp_free_ram = data['free_ram']
694→                                    m.esp_total_ram = data['total_ram']
695→                                    m.esp_free_rom = data['free_rom']
696→                                    m.esp_total_rom = data['total_rom']
697→                                    m.esp_cpu_freq = data['cpu_freq']
698→                                    m.esp_wifi_ssid = data['wifi_ssid']
699→                                    m.esp_wifi_rssi = data['wifi_rssi']
700→                                    db.commit()
701→                            finally:
702→                                db.close()
703→                    except Exception as e:
704→                        print(f"Serial Loop Error: {e}")
705→                        break
706→        except Exception:
707→            time.sleep(2)
708→
709→@app.on_event("startup")
710→async def start_serial_monitor():
711→    thread = threading.Thread(target=serial_monitor_task, daemon=True)
712→    thread.start()
713→
714→# ─── WEBSOCKET ───
715→@app.websocket("/ws/machines")
716→async def websocket_endpoint(websocket: WebSocket):
717→    await websocket.accept()
718→    active_connections.append(websocket)
719→    try:
720→        while True:
721→            db = database.SessionLocal()
722→            try:
723→                machines = db.query(models.Machine).all()
724→                data = []
725→                for m in machines:
726→                    remaining = max(0.0, m.initial_asnova_length - (m.current_total_meters - m.meters_at_fill))
727→                    now = datetime.utcnow()
728→                    is_esp_online = m.last_seen and (now - m.last_seen).total_seconds() < 60
729→                    if not is_esp_online:
730→                        status = "OFFLINE"
731→                    elif m.current_baud == 0:
732→                        status = "ESP_ONLINE_NO_SIGNAL"
733→                    elif remaining <= 0:
734→                        status = "ASNOVA_EMPTY"
735→                    else:
736→                        status = "RUNNING"
737→                    data.append({
738→                        "id": m.id, "status": status,
739→                        "meters": round(m.current_total_meters, 2),
740→                        "shift_meters": round(m.shift_meters, 2),
741→                        "remaining": round(remaining, 2),
742→                        "baud": m.current_baud, "protocol": m.current_protocol,
743→                        "connection_source": m.connection_source,
744→                        "preferred_source": m.preferred_source,
745→                        "sys_info": {
746→                            "ram": {"free": m.esp_free_ram, "total": m.esp_total_ram},
747→                            "rom": {"free": m.esp_free_rom, "total": m.esp_total_rom},
748→                            "cpu": m.esp_cpu_freq,
749→                            "wifi": {"ssid": m.esp_wifi_ssid, "rssi": m.esp_wifi_rssi}
750→                        }
751→                    })
752→                await websocket.send_text(json.dumps(data))
753→            finally:
754→                db.close()
755→            await asyncio.sleep(2)
756→    except WebSocketDisconnect:
757→        pass
758→    except Exception as e:
759→        print(f"WS Error: {e}")
760→    finally:
761→        if websocket in active_connections:
762→            active_connections.remove(websocket)
763→
